terms.definition: A large-scale machine learning model trained on broad datasets that can be adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks. Examples include Claude, Titan, and Llama models available through Amazon Bedrock.

A technique that combines information retrieval with text generation. The system retrieves relevant documents from a knowledge base and provides them as context to the FM, improving accuracy and reducing hallucination.
A numerical representation of text (or other data) in a high-dimensional space where semantically similar items are closer together. Used for semantic search in RAG systems.
The practice of crafting effective instructions for foundation models to achieve desired outputs. Includes techniques like few-shot learning, chain-of-thought, and system prompts.
Safety controls that filter FM inputs and outputs. Can block harmful content, denied topics, and PII. Amazon Bedrock Guardrails provides managed implementation.
Autonomous AI systems that can plan, reason, and execute actions to accomplish tasks. They use foundation models for decision-making and can invoke external tools and APIs.
In Amazon Bedrock Agents, collections of actions that the agent can perform. Each action is backed by a Lambda function and defined by an OpenAPI schema.
The process of further training a foundation model on domain-specific data to improve performance for particular tasks. Amazon Bedrock supports continued pre-training and fine-tuning.
When an FM generates plausible-sounding but incorrect or unsupported information. Mitigation strategies include RAG, grounding instructions, and source attribution.
The basic unit of text processing for LLMs. A token is roughly 4 characters or 0.75 words in English. Token counts determine costs and context limits.
The maximum number of tokens an FM can process in a single request, including both input and output. Larger windows allow more context but increase costs.
A managed service for RAG in Amazon Bedrock. It handles document ingestion, chunking, embedding generation, vector storage, and retrieval.
A pricing model in Amazon Bedrock that provides dedicated capacity with guaranteed performance and lower per-token costs for sustained workloads.
Detailed logging of Bedrock FM interactions including prompts, responses, and parameters. Essential for debugging, quality analysis, and compliance.
An evaluation technique where one FM evaluates outputs from another FM. Provides scalable, nuanced quality assessment for helpfulness, accuracy, and other criteria.
A prompting technique that instructs the model to show its reasoning process step-by-step. Improves accuracy on complex tasks and provides transparency.
Caching FM responses based on query meaning rather than exact text match. Uses embedding similarity to identify semantically equivalent queries.
Routing queries to different model tiers based on complexity. Simple queries go to cheaper, faster models while complex queries go to more capable models.
A security attack where malicious user input attempts to override system instructions. Mitigated through input validation, clear prompt structure, and guardrails.
Private connectivity between a VPC and AWS services without using the public internet. Interface VPC Endpoints (PrivateLink) provide private access to Amazon Bedrock.
Standardized documentation for AI models describing capabilities, limitations, intended uses, and ethical considerations. SageMaker Model Cards provide programmatic management.
Returning FM responses incrementally as they generate rather than waiting for completion. Improves perceived latency for long-form generation.
An open protocol for connecting AI assistants to external tools and data sources. Enables dynamic tool discovery without application code changes.
A reasoning-action framework where agents alternate between reasoning about what to do and taking actions. Bedrock Agents use ReAct-style orchestration.
Constraining FM outputs to be based on provided context or retrieved documents. Reduces hallucination by ensuring answers come from verified sources.
